title: Resurrection
creator: Tintoretto, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1519--1594
description: Depicts the resurrection of Christ. Laymen relax in the foreground, soldiers run to the left of Christ, who is central in the work with angels behind him. In the background, two figures with cloth around their heads discuss intently.
subject: Resurrection--Art; Jesus Christ; Christianity
description_provenance: Sir William Farrar (owner); Ashmolean Museum (owner/exhibitor)

title: Venetian Senator (Marco Zani)
creator: Tintoretto, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1519--1594
description: Portrait of a Venetian Senator possbily named Marco Zani. The Senator stands regally, in dress robes and looks outwards towards the viewer. He has long white hair and beard.
subject: Portrait; Venice (Italy)--Civilization--16th century; Politicians
description_provenance: Bought in Italy by Charles Stirling before 1826 (owner/exhibitor); Sotheby's sale, July 3, 1963, lot 46 (auctioned)

title: Holy Family & Infant St. John
creator: Bordone, Paris (Artist; visual artist) 1500--1571
description: In the right foreground, Mary lies down on her side with a book. She is looking at a blank space, between the book on the right and a sheep on the left, behind which infants Jesus and John the Baptist are playing. In the background, there is a figure riding on horseback.
subject: Mary, Blessed Virgin, Saint; Jesus Christ; John, the Baptist, Saint--Art
description_provenance: Casa Venier, Venice. Sir Phillip Stephens (owner); Christie's sale, May 17, 1810, lot 77 (auctioned); Lord Ranelagh, 1829 (owner); Stirling College, Keir (owner/exhibitor); Stirling Sale, Sotheby's, July 3, 1963, lot 44 (auctioned); Christie's sale, Dec. 10, 1982, lot 60 (auctioned)

title: Rest in Feight
creator: Bordone, Paris (Artist; visual artist) 1500--1571
description: This painting depicts a group of people, one man along with women and children, resting in the forest, with rucksacks and vases near them. The prescence of angels on the right side of the work suggests this is a religious painting representing a Biblical story.
subject: Christianity; Relaxation; Angels -- Art
description_provenance: James Cozemore sale, Christie's, May 18, 1833, lot 105 (auctioned); Spencer Churchill, Northwick Park (owner/exhibitor); R.A. number 76, 1960 (exhibited); Christie's Sale, May 28, 1965, lot 27 (auctioned); Christie's Sale, March 14, 1975, lot 110 (auctioned)

title: Front (Rest in Feight)
source: G. Canova, pp. 13-14, 109, repr. Antologica di Belle Arti (September 3, 1977)

title: 3/4 Length Portrait of fair haired woman holding elaborate mirror
creator: Bordone, Paris (Artist; visual artist) 1500--1571
description: Portrait of a woman with long fair hair holidng an elaborate hand mirror next a wooden armoire. The woman is looking to the viewer's right.
subject: Portrait; Hand Mirrors; Woman
description_provenance: Burlington House, 1873, number 134 (exhibited); Burlington House, 1904, number 73 (exhibited); Chiristie's sale, Foudou(?), July 24, 1945, bought by Koesten for 650 pounds (auctioned); Earl of Radnor, Longford Caslte (exhibited)

title: Front (3/4 Length Portrait of fair haired woman holding elaborate mirror)
Verso (3/4 Length Portrait of fair haired woman holding elaborate mirror)
Astronomy
creator: Tintoretto, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1519--1594
description: This painting depicts a seated woman wearing a semi-sheer dress holding an armillary sphere affixed to a handle. The background depicts a landscape with trees.
subject: armillary spheres; astronomy; trees
description_provenance: Cook, Richmond, (Surrey) as Paolo Veronese, cat. no. 176 (owner); Fred Mont, New York, February 1955 (owner); Private collection, Germany, acquired from the above (owner)

title: Portrait of Old Woman
creator: Bassano, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1510--1592
description: Portrait of an old woman wearing a long-sleaved dress, sitting in a chair holding a small book.
subject: portraits; old age; chair; books; dresses (garments)
description_provenance: Oliver Vernon Watney, Cornbury Park, Charlbury, Oxfordshire (owner); Christie's, 23 June 1967, lot 37 (auctioned); Christie's, 11 July 1975, lot 182 (auctioned); Sotheby's, New York, 11 June 1981, lot 122 (auctioned)

title: [Madonna and Child]
creator: Bassano, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1510--1592
description: This painting depicts Madonna and child gazing down at a cherub holding a staff. The bottom left corner stands a lamb looking towards the cherub.
subject: lambs; crosses
description_provenance: Canessa, Milan, sold by 1954 (owner)

title: The Astronomer
creator: Veronese, Paolo (Artist; visual artist) 1528--1588
description: This painting depicts a man in a turban leaning against a wall holding an armillary sphere affixed to a handle. The background depicts clouds and foliage.
subject: armillary spheres; turbans; clouds; leaves
description_provenance: De Burlet, Berlin, September 1922 (owner); Agnew, August 1925 (owner); Sir Stephen Courtauld, Umtali, Zimbabwe (owner)

title: Shepherd & Nymph
creator: Bordone, Paris (Artist; visual artist) 1500--1571
description: A painting depicting a nude man laying near a tree with his clothing draped over a branch, a semi-draped woman is close by. Above the two figures, a cupid flys over them with two flower wreathes.
subject: nudity; cupids; landscapes; trees; flowers
description_provenance: Crespi Collection, Milan (owner)

title: Portrait of Caterina Varano
creator: Piombo, Sebastiano del (Artist; visual artist) 1485--1547
description: A woman stands at the center of the painting, her right arm outstretched and resting on a Catherine wheel. She stands with her right side facing the viewer, and her face is turned slightly towards us. She is dressed as Saint Catherine of Alexandria, in a Renaissance-style dress and veil.
subject: woman; dress
description_provenance: Louvre Museum, Paris (owner)

title: Rest on Flight (variant of the Ambrosiana)
creator: Bassano, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1510--1592
description: Mary sits at the center of the composition, holding the infant Jesus. Joseph rests on the ground next to her. Other figures surround the couple, including a donkey in the upper left corner of the image. The group is situated next to a tree.
subject: Madonna; Saint Joseph; donkey
description_provenance: Acquired in Florence c. 1871 by Sir John Leslie (owner); Desmond Leslie, Castle Leslie, Co. Monaghan, Eire (owner); Sotheby's sale, Dec. 12, 1973, lot 84 (auctioned)

title: Front (Rest on Flight (variant of the Ambrosiana))
Verso (Rest on Flight (variant of the Ambrosiana))
Rest on Flight
creator: Bordone, Paris (Artist; visual artist) 1500--1571
description: Mary and Joseph sit next to a stream, with hilly countryside stretching into the background. Joseph holds the infant Jesus. A donkey grazes in the far left of the image
subject: Madonna; Saint Joseph; donkey
description_provenance: Count Adam Potocki, Krakow (owner); Count Antoine Seilern, London (owner)

title: Courtesan
creator: Bordone, Paris (Artist; visual artist) 1500--1571
description: A woman with long, flowing hair stands in front of a row of columns. A bit of cloudy sky is visible through a window in the upper right corner of the work. The woman wears a loose, flowing garment and cloak, and one of her breasts is exposed.
subject: woman; dress; architecture
description_provenance: Oliver Vernon Watney, Cornbury Park, Charlbury, Oxfordshire (owner); Christie's, 23 June 1967, lot 57 (auctioned); J. Paul Getty, Sutton Place, Guildford, Surrey (owner); J. Paul Getty Museum, Malibu, 1978. Christie's sale, New York, 21 May 1992 lot 37 (auctioned)

title: Poet between Vice & Virtue
creator: Veronese, Paolo (Artist; visual artist) 1528--1588
description: A man stands between two women in dresses, each pulling him in opposite directions. One woman has her back to the viewer, and a sphinx and dagger are hidden behind her skirt. A stone statue of a woman looks down from the upper left corner, and in the upper right is a tree with green foliage. A blue sky with white clouds stretches over the scene.
subject: man; woman; mythology
description_provenance: Ravolta Barme von Zur Muehler, London (owner); Edith von Zur Muehler, Ponte a Moriano, Lucca (owner)

title: Portrait of a Venetian General
creator: Veronese, Paolo (Artist; visual artist) 1528--1588
description: Portrait of a Venetian General standing contrapposto with a sword at his waistband, leaning on a cloaked pedestal that holds up his helmet.
subject: man; portrait; Venice (Italy); armor
description_provenance: The 12th Duke of Hamilton, Hamilton Palace (owner); Christie Sale, June 24, 1882 lot 411 as Giorgione (auctioned); The 6th Earl of Rosebery, Mentmore (owner); Christie Sale, May 5, 1939 lot 45 as Giorgione (auctioned); Koetser (owner); Madame K.L. (owner); Gal. Charpentier Sale, Paris, Nov 30, 1954 lot 34 (auctioned); Wildenstein (owner); Frederick W. Field, Greenacres, Beverly Hills (owner); Christie Sale, London, July 5, 1991 lot 4. (auctioned)

title: Venus & Adonis
creator: Veronese, Paolo (Artist; visual artist) 1528--1588
description: Adonis holding Venus by her breast as she falls backward over him, with Cupid peeking from under her. Adonis holds onto a rope in his other hand coming from three dogs.
subject: Venus; Adonis; Cupid; mythology
description_provenance: Private Coll.

title: Front (Venus & Adonis)
source: Pallucchini, Acropoli, 1963 pp. 293-303, repr. in color

title: Madonna with Infant John
creator: Bassano, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1510--1592
description: Madonna and child seated, with Madonna with attention towards an Infant John holding a staff.
subject: Mary; Saint John the Baptist
description_provenance: Earl of Carlisle (owner); Ct. Contini. Edenbridge (Kent) Archibald Werner (owner); Sotheby Sale, March 27, 1968 lot 27 (auctioned); The New Gallery, 1894-5 (exhibited); Agnew's, June 1952 (no. 2) (exhibited); Venice 1957 n.52 (exhibited)

title: Front (Madonna with Infant John)
source: Boll'd'Arte. VIII. 1929 (2). pp. 407-413. Arslan. fig. 27. I.p.167

title: Madonna & Child & St John
creator: Bassano, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1510--1592
description: Madonna and child seated, with Madonna with attention towards an Infant John holding a staff.
subject: Mary; Saint John the Baptist
description_provenance: Lord Carlisle, Castle Howard (owner); Hon. Geoffrey W. A. Howard 1944 (owner); Edenbridge (Kent). Archibald Werner. Sotheby Sale, March 27, 1968 lot 27 (auctioned); Venice 1957 n. 52 (exhibited)

title: Resurrection
creator: Tintoretto, Jacopo (Artist; visual artist) 1519--1594
description: Jesus Christ ressurrects, floating above three men laying in wait around a tomb.
subject: Jesus Christ; resurrection; tomb
description_provenance: Da Mula, 1861 (owner); William Graham, 1886 (owner); Charles Butler, 1911 (owner); M. von Nemes (owner); Dr. Alsburg Coll. Berlin (owner); Daly Coll., 1925. Sold at Sotheby 8.12.50 for 1400 guineas (auctioned); Sotheby Sale, April 1967 lot 39 (auctioned)

title: Front (Resurrection)
source: Catalogue of Nemes Sale, Paris 1913. Photo sent by Wildenstein 24.11.50.
